items.title: WC final tickets listed on FIFA resale at over $2M
items.summary: FIFA's resale site has four tickets on sale for the World Cup final for just under $2.3 million each.

items.title: Amnesty Int'l issues WC travel advisory for U.S.
items.summary: Amnesty International and dozens of U.S. civil and human rights groups issued a "World Cup travel advisory" Thursday, warning tournament visitors of "rising authoritarianism and increasing violence" in the United States.

items.title: Italy rejects 'shameful' idea to replace Iran at WC
items.summary: Italian sports officials say four-time champion Italy is not interested in replacing Iran at the upcoming World Cup following a suggestion to that effect by a Trump administration official.

items.title: Le Club de Bruges espérait son retour, mais il a refusé : "Ma carrière n'est pas encore terminée"
items.summary: En fin de contrat dans deux mois à Courtrai, Brecht Dejaegere aimerait disputer une dernière saison en Jupiler Pro League avant de raccrocher. Une raison pour laquelle le milieu de terrain de 34 ans a refusé la proposition du Club de Bruges.

items.title: Mentale voordeel weer bij Club Brugge? Dit denkt Van De Perre ervan
items.summary: Union SG wist deze week niet te winnen van KAA Gent. Daardoor komt Club Brugge opnieuw op minder dan een wedstrijd achterstand van de regerende landskampioen.

items.title: Millwall receive apology over use of club logo in racism booklet, Bob Wilson rails at Football Focus axe: football – live
items.summary: ⚽ All the latest football news heading into a huge weekend ⚽ Fixtures | Tables | Get the Football Daily email | Mail Yara Here’s your quiz of the week, see if you can beat my 10/15. Freddie Woodman’s dad, Andy, had to watch his Bromley team lose from the stands at Salford last night. A big home win for Gary Neville’s club, Paul Scholes was watching on, and automatic promotion might be on. Bromley have to wait and see if they win the League Two title. So much to play for in the EFL. Continue reading...

items.title: Howe 'looks forward' to talks with Newcastle owners
items.summary: Head coach Eddie Howe says he is looking forward to sitting down with Newcastle United's owners next week to "express things from my perspective".

items.title: Roma-eigenaren kiezen kant van Gasperini: Ranieri stopt als adviseur
items.summary: Claudio Ranieri heeft een stap opzij gezet bij AS Roma. De 74-jarige Romein had in zijn rol als adviseur een openlijke aanvaring met trainer Gian Piero Gasperini, waardoor een onwerkbare situatie was ontstaan.

items.title: Après sa carrière, Simon Mignolet fera comme Jan Vertonghen et aidera le football belge
items.summary: Jan Vertonghen a décidé d'aider le développement du football belge depuis la fin de sa carrière. Il sera rejoint, en septembre, par Simon Mignolet, qu'il n'hésitera pas à épauler.

items.title: Transfernieuws en Transfergeruchten 24/04: tolaj
items.summary: De transfergeruchten, het laatste transfernieuws en officiële transfers vliegen ons om de oren. Bij Voetbalkrant zetten wij alles voor u op een rijtje zodat u tijdens deze mercato niets hoeft te missen! Op 24/04 hebben we transfernieuws en transfergeruchten voor u over:

items.title: Eindelijk de ideale match? 'Anderlecht volgt spits uit... derde klasse'
items.summary: RSC Anderlecht slaagde er tegen STVV niet in om te scoren. Ondanks verschillende opties blijft paars-wit duidelijk de markt afschuimen op zoek naar offensieve versterking.

items.title: Rondje Eredivisie: Feyenoord in onzekerheid en PSV naar de zon
items.summary: Tijd voor een nieuwe aflevering van Rondje Eredivisie. We blikken natuurlijk terug op een aantal midweekse wedstrijden. Zo won PSV ruim van PEC Zwolle en was Telstar te sterk voor Sparta, maar ook komend weekend wordt er gewoon weer gespeeld. Kan Feyenoord goede zaken doen in de strijd om plek twee? Of kan Ajax nog dichterbij komen? Clubwatchers Tim Reedijk en Sander Janssen bespreken het aan tafel.

items.title: Italië verklaart liefde aan gedroomde bondscoach Guardiola
items.summary: Pep Guardiola staat vrijdag op de voorpagina van La Gazzetta dello Sport . In Italië hopen ze namelijk vurig dat de Spaanse succestrainer besluit om Manchester City te verlaten om bondscoach van de Azzurri te worden, al lijkt de wens momenteel nog de vader van de gedachte te zijn. 'Dromen kost niets.'

items.title: Hein Vanhaezebrouck vindt het, maar krijgt Union daadwerkelijk te weinig respect?
items.summary: Union SG maakte vorig weekend een statement door Club Brugge te verslaan. Vooral de manier waarop was indrukwekkend. Maar volgens Hein Vanhaezebrouck krijgen de Brusselaars nog altijd niet het respect dat ze verdienen.

items.title: Schreuder ziet goede reactie bij NEC en weet wie Önal vervangt tegen Twente
items.summary: Dick Schreuder heeft een goede reactie gezien bij NEC na de verloren bekerfinale tegen AZ (5-1). De trainer van de Nijmegenaren vertelde zondag na de afstraffing in De Kuip dat hij iedereen woensdag weer met een lach op zijn gezicht op de club wilde zien .

items.title: Pas de finale de la Croky Cup à la télévision : voici le montant que DAZN réclamait pour partager les droits
items.summary: Conformément au décret national relatif aux médias, la finale de la Coupe de Belgique doit être diffusée à la télévision. Ce ne sera cependant pas le cas cette année, DAZN ayant gardé la pleine possession des droits.

items.title: Slot aangewezen op derde doelman Liverpool, maar Alisson op de weg terug
items.summary: Freddie Woodman zal zaterdag onder de lat staan als Liverpool het opneemt tegen Crystal Palace. Manager Arne Slot hoopt wel snel weer te beschikken over doelman Alisson, terwijl Giorgi Mamardashvili voorlopig niet beschikbaar is.

items.title: How Lineker and Neville became rival media moguls
items.summary: BBC sports editor Dan Roan examines how Gary Lineker and Gary Neville became two of the biggest players in a rapidly changing media industry.

items.title: "Wij zijn Anderlecht, geen klein ploegje": Adriano Bertaccini slaat met de vuist op tafel
items.summary: Anderlecht ging met 2-0 onderuit op Stayen tegen STVV en ziet zo de derde plaats uit handen glippen. Bertaccini baalt van de ingesteldheid van zijn ploeg en roept op om snel te reageren.

items.title: ‘It’s gone all over’: Southampton’s Shea Charles on his viral celebration and FA Cup dream
items.summary: After scoring the goal that knocked out Arsenal, the midfielder is relishing Saturday’s semi-final against his old club Manchester City In the seconds after Southampton disposed of Arsenal to tee up an FA Cup semi-final with Manchester City, a camera operator scooted on to the St Mary’s pitch and got to work on locating the match-winner. As the crowd swayed to the sound of Doris Day’s Que Sera, Sera, another lasting image was born. In between high-fiving and embracing teammates, Shea Charles tilted his head and turned towards the camera, raising his eyebrows a little with a playful ‘how-about-that-then?’ expression. It was a snapshot that snowballed into a viral meme, viewed by millions on social media, and a couple of days later Southampton asked their players to recreate the moment. “I just looked at the camera as if I was looking at my mates down the lens,” Charles says. “I’ve seen it’s gone all over.” Continue reading...

items.title: Rule change could pave way for Véron Mosengo-Omba to become president of DRC football federation
items.summary: Former Caf general secretary sets sights on Fecofa role Swiss national is university friend of Gianni Infantino Football’s governing body in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (the DRC) has changed eligibility rules for its presidential elections, allowing a university friend of Gianni Infantino to stand. Véron Mosengo-Omba, who was at university with the Fifa president in Switzerland, is a former general secretary of the Confederation of African Football (Caf), and confirmed his candidacy to be president of the DRC’s football federation, Fecofa, this week. Continue reading...

items.title: Cela se décante pour Cédric Hatenboer, dont l'avenir s'écrira bien loin du Sporting d'Anderlecht
items.summary: N'ayant jamais reçu sa chance à Anderlecht, Cédric Hatenboer s'éclate aujourd'hui à Telstar, et ne veut logiquement pas revenir au Lotto Park. D'autant que plusieurs clubs d'Eredivisie se montrent déjà intéressés par ses services.

items.title: L'ancien meilleur buteur de Pro League ouvre grand la porte... au Standard : "Oui, j'aimerais bien"
items.summary: Formé au Standard, Hugo Cuypers n'a jamais eu l'occasion de jouer avec l'équipe première. Un rêve qu'il aimerait réaliser avant la fin de sa carrière.

items.title: Millwall consider legal action over badge on KKK image
items.summary: Millwall consider taking legal action after Westminster City Council used the club's badge on an illustration of a Ku Klux Klan member.

items.title: Weekend predictions: Will Rosenior exit stop Chelsea slide? Arsenal's collapse to continue?
items.summary: Chelsea play their first match post-Rosenior, while Arsenal try to rebound from a crushing loss in the Premier League title race. Julien Laurens makes his predictions.

items.title: What's at stake in Europe's top soccer leagues: Titles, UCL qualification, relegation
items.summary: How are the battles for major honors, Champions League spots and relegation shaping up across the continent?

items.title: US says Iran players welcome at 2026 World Cup amid call for Italy to take their place
items.summary: No one from US ‘told them they can’t come’, Rubio says Delegation members with ties to IRGC may be barred Iran’s footballers will be welcome at this year’s World Cup, secretary of state Marco Rubio said Thursday, distancing the United States government from a proposal that Italy could take their place in the tournament. Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, Rubio denied that the government had asked the Iranian team not to come to the World Cup – but warned the US may yet bar entry to members of the Iranian delegation it judged to have ties to Tehran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), which is regarded as a terrorist organisation by Washington and several other governments. Continue reading...

items.title: Who will win the title? Reporters, fans and pundits make their predictions
items.summary: Will it be Manchester City or Arsenal for the Premier League title? BBC Sport gets pundits, reporters and fans to predict each of their remaining fixtures - and who will win the league.

items.title: Premier League and FA Cup semi-finals: 10 things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Spurs face must-win game at Wolves, Arsenal fight to keep title hopes alive and Chelsea step into a post-Rosenior world Maybe it is a case of fourth time lucky for Nottingham Forest. Certainly Vítor Pereira – manager No 4 in the most chaotic of seasons – is doing something right. The Europa League semi-finalists are unbeaten in their last five Premier League games and will arrive at the Stadium of Light knowing victory would move them within touching distance of safety. Their visit should provide an interesting tactical challenge for Sunderland. Régis Le Bris’ side often excel on the counterattack but Forest are likely to sit deep and invite their hosts to unpick their packed defence while hoping to hurt them on the break. Le Bris will surely need Nordi Mukiele to advance with typical verve from right-back, while, in midfield, Noah Sadiki and Enzo Le Fée will be required to demonstrate precisely why they are being watched by several leading clubs. This Sunderland team often play with real and refreshing personality. Can Forest subdue them? Louise Taylor Sunderland v Nottingham Forest, Premier League, Friday 8pm (all times BST) Fulham v Aston Villa, Premier League, Saturday 12.30pm West Ham v Everton, Premier League, Saturday 3pm Wolves v Tottenham, Premier League, Saturday 3pm Continue reading...

items.title: Big clubs and big names - a summer of managerial upheaval awaits
items.summary: BBC Sport senior football correspondent Sami Mokbel analyses a summer that promises plenty of change in the Premier League.
